name: Salto del Fraile y el castillo de Castro Ferral - circular gravel through the P.N. of Despeñaperros
name_original: Salto del Fraile y el castillo de Castro Ferral - circular gravel por el P.N. de Despeñaperros
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Santa Elena are characterized by diverse terrain, including mountain biking trails with root sections and rock gardens, and fast forest paths. The region features the Parque Natural Despeñaperros, offering gravel cycling opportunities. Routes often involve significant elevation gains, providing varied challenges for cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Santa Elena The most popular touring cycling route is Salto del Fraile y el castillo de Castro Ferral - circular gravel through the P.N. of Despeñaperros, a 18.9 miles (30.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 58 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers a circular gravel experience through the Parque Natural Despeñaperros. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Parque Natural Despeñaperros — circular de gravel desde Coto Sierra del Viso, a difficult 24.3 miles (39.1 km) path. This route provides a circular gravel experience within the Parque Natural Despeñaperros. Local touring cyclists also love the Miranda del Rey – Roman Bridge in Despeñaperros loop from Santa Elena, a 27.2 miles (43.8 km) trail leading through the Parque Natural Despeñaperros, often completed in about 2 hours 44 minutes. Touring cycling around Santa Elena is defined by diverse terrain, gravel paths, and significant elevation changes. The network offers options for different ability levels, though many routes are challenging. The routes in Santa Elena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 23 reviews. More than 400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Santa Elena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Santa Elena?
A: Touring cycling routes around Santa Elena, particularly within the Parque Natural Despeñaperros, feature diverse terrain. You'll encounter gravel paths, mountain biking sections with root sections and rock gardens, and fast forest paths. Many routes involve significant elevation gains, offering varied challenges.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes in Santa Elena suitable for beginners?
A: While Santa Elena offers a wide range of cycling routes, the majority are classified as difficult, with 27 out of 34 routes falling into this category. There are 7 moderate routes, but currently, no easy touring cycling routes are listed. Cyclists should be prepared for challenging terrain and significant elevation changes.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in Santa Elena?
A: Yes, Santa Elena offers several excellent circular touring cycling routes. For example, you can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994124071/salto-del-fraile-y-el-castillo-de-castro-ferral-circular-gravel-through-the-p-n-of-despenaperros" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto del Fraile y el castillo de Castro Ferral - circular gravel through the P.N. of Despeñaperros&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375490018/parque-natural-despenaperros-circular-de-gravel-desde-coto-sierra-del-viso" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural Despeñaperros — circular de gravel desde Coto Sierra del Viso&lt;/a&gt;. Another popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38167258" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miranda del Rey – Roman Bridge in Despeñaperros loop from Santa Elena&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural attractions or viewpoints can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Santa Elena is rich in natural beauty and offers several viewpoints and natural monuments. Along your routes, you might encounter highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5620563" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balcón de Despeñaperros Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5418434" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desfiladero Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4215341" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de los Órganos&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features impressive waterfalls like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5289848" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco del Negrillo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5337911" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto del Fraile Waterfall and Spring&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by historical landmarks?
A: Yes, some routes incorporate historical elements. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38167258" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miranda del Rey – Roman Bridge in Despeñaperros loop from Santa Elena&lt;/a&gt; passes by the Roman Bridge. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5358665" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molino del Batán&lt;/a&gt;, an old mill, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5418382" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cimbarra Mill&lt;/a&gt;, which are interesting points of interest along various routes.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Santa Elena?
A: The touring cycling routes in Santa Elena are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 23 reviews. More than 400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore the area, often praising the diverse terrain and challenging nature of the trails within the Parque Natural Despeñaperros.

Q: What is the typical length and elevation gain for touring cycling routes in Santa Elena?
A: Routes in Santa Elena vary in length, with many being quite substantial. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994124071/salto-del-fraile-y-el-castillo-de-castro-ferral-circular-gravel-through-the-p-n-of-despenaperros" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto del Fraile y el castillo de Castro Ferral&lt;/a&gt; route is 18.9 miles (30.4 km) with over 600 meters of elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375490018/parque-natural-despenaperros-circular-de-gravel-desde-coto-sierra-del-viso" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural Despeñaperros — circular de gravel desde Coto Sierra del Viso&lt;/a&gt; is 24.3 miles (39.1 km) with over 1100 meters of ascent. Cyclists should be prepared for significant distances and considerable climbing.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling options in Santa Elena?
A: Given that the majority of touring cycling routes in Santa Elena are classified as difficult, and there are no easy routes, options for young children or families seeking very gentle rides might be limited. However, some of the moderate routes could be suitable for families with older, experienced children who are comfortable with varied terrain and elevation changes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Santa Elena?
A: Santa Elena, near Medellín, Colombia, generally experiences a mild climate year-round due to its elevation. However, for touring cycling, it's often best to avoid the heaviest rainy seasons to ensure more comfortable riding conditions on unpaved sections. The dry season typically offers clearer skies and more stable terrain, but specific weather patterns can vary. Always check local forecasts before heading out.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that are dog-friendly in Santa Elena?
A: While the natural parks and trails in Santa Elena are generally welcoming to outdoor activities, specific regulations regarding dogs on cycling routes can vary. It's advisable to check with local park authorities or specific trail guidelines within the Parque Natural Despeñaperros to confirm if dogs are permitted and if any restrictions (like leashes) apply on the routes you plan to cycle.

Q: What is the longest touring cycling route available in Santa Elena?
A: One of the longer touring cycling routes in Santa Elena is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24523890" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Empedraillo – Miranda del Rey loop from Santa Elena&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 34.5 miles (55.6 km) and includes over 1300 meters of elevation gain. This route is classified as difficult and offers a substantial challenge for experienced cyclists.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are there in Santa Elena?
A: There are over 30 touring cycling routes available around Santa Elena, offering a wide variety of experiences for cyclists. The network includes 7 moderate routes and 27 difficult routes, ensuring options for different skill levels, though many are quite challenging.
